description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM are not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is a chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique ports for each service across the system.

description: In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

description: In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - The implementation uses a software-based approach to track start and stop times for secure channels and associations. Consequently, the stop times are only updated when the secure channel or association is deleted, and thus are not reflected in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. description: Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out

description: Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

description: Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

description: Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream.
workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

description: Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: The /var/log/ directory disk buffer is almost full or the log buffer is full and new log messages are not being logged locally. Scenario: When ERPS is configured, ERPS logs are accumulated excessively. Workaround: Manually remove the ERPS log files or reboot the switch.

description: Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): The documented workaround is: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

description: Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

messages.content: What is the symptom of Bug 353178?

REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays an error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device. Scenario: When console logging is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process completes but generates an HTTP 500 error. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, disable console logging by removing 'logging console' from the configuration and attempt the firmware upload again, or use TFTP for firmware upload.

description: Symptom: DHCP server configuration status becomes stuck in a pending state after configuration changes are pushed from Aruba Central, preventing proper DHCP service operation. Scenario: When administrators make configuration changes using Aruba Central, either through Central template groups or the UI group multi-edit option, the DHCP server configuration status may become stuck in a pending state. This issue can occur even when the configuration changes are completely unrelated to DHCP services. The pending status persists indefinitely, requiring manual intervention.
